content.subheading: Things to Do in Tigoni: A Complete Guide to Kenya’s Most Scenic Countryside Escape
content.intro: Often described as Africa’s richest village, Tigoni is a lush highland region in Limuru, just under an hour from Nairobi. Known for its rolling tea plantations, cool climate, historic estates, and quietly luxurious pace of life, Tigoni offers far more than a simple countryside getaway.
paragraphs: Often described as Africa’s richest village, Tigoni is a lush highland region in Limuru, just under an hour from Nairobi. Known for its rolling tea plantations, cool climate, historic estates, and quietly luxurious pace of life, Tigoni offers far more than a simple countryside getaway. It is a place to slow down, explore, and experience Kenya in its most refined natural form.

From immersive tea farm walks and ebike Tours to destination dining, wellness retreats, and family-friendly outdoor experiences, Tigoni has become one of the most rewarding places to visit near Nairobi. Below is a curated guide to the best things to do in Tigoni.
Tea Farm Walks & Tea Experiences in Tigoni

Tigoni is the birthplace of Kenya’s tea industry, with tea first grown here experimentally in 1903 and the first commercial estate established in 1910. Walking through these estates is both scenic and deeply historic.

Ravenswood Farm offers serene walks through expansive tea fields and indigenous trees, making it ideal for slow nature walks, photography, and dog-friendly strolls. The quiet paths and elevated views give visitors a true sense of Tigoni’s agricultural heritage.

Kiambethu Tea Farm, one of Kenya’s oldest tea farms, provides guided tea experiences that combine plantation walks, tea education, and tastings. Visitors learn how tea is grown, harvested, and processed while enjoying panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

Gathoni Tea Farm is known for its purple tea and small-scale, sustainable approach. Tours here feel personal and educational, offering insight into modern tea innovation while remaining rooted in Tigoni’s traditions.
Little Falls Tea Garden combines tea gardens with riverside scenery and gentle walking trails, making it a peaceful option for visitors looking to blend nature, water features, and tea landscapes.
Tea farm walks in Tigoni are ideal for couples, small groups, photographers, and anyone seeking calm, educational outdoor experiences.

gallery.attribution: Photo by Jane Smith on Unsplash
Photo by Alex Green on Unsplash

closing: eBike Tours in Tigoni

One of the most exciting and accessible ways to explore Tigoni is through a guided eBike tour. With the help of electric-assisted bicycles, visitors can cover more ground comfortably while still enjoying the fresh air, scenic views, and quiet countryside that define the region. eBike tours are suitable for all fitness levels, making them ideal for beginners, families with older children, couples, and groups.

eBike routes in Tigoni wind through rolling tea plantations, forest paths, rural backroads, and scenic viewpoints, offering a deeper look into the landscape beyond what you can see by car. The electric boost makes it easy to tackle Tigoni’s hills, allowing riders to focus on the experience rather than the effort.

Most tours are guided by locals who share insights about Tigoni’s history, tea culture, and hidden corners, turning the ride into both an adventure and a storytelling experience. Tours typically start from scenic countryside locations and run at different times of day, making them easy to fit into a day trip or weekend itinerary.
Nature Trails & Outdoor Walks
Tigoni’s elevation—approximately 2,100 metres above sea level—creates a cool climate and lush environment that is perfect for walking, hiking, and trail exploration.

The Brackenhurst Forest Trail is one of the most popular nature walks in the area. It winds through indigenous forest, offering shaded paths, birdsong, and a tranquil atmosphere ideal for nature lovers and beginner hikers.
The Ravenswood Trail runs alongside tea fields and forest edges, offering open views and gentle terrain. It is especially popular for relaxed hikes, dog walks, and sunset strolls.
The Limuru Tea Estate Trail takes visitors deeper into working tea estates, where rolling green hills stretch as far as the eye can see. This trail offers a classic Tigoni landscape experience.
The Kwamwaki Trail is more secluded and rural, passing through countryside paths and local farmland. It is perfect for visitors seeking quieter, less-traveled routes with an authentic local feel.
Food & Cafés in Tigoni
Tigoni has quietly become one of Kenya’s most exciting countryside food destinations, offering everything from farm-to-table cafés to refined fusion dining.

The Fig & Olive Restaurant. This iconic Tigoni Cafe is loved for its relaxed elegance, dog-friendly policy, and family appeal. Its children’s section, Amuse, allows kids to paint and create while adults enjoy long lunches by the fireplace or in the garden.

Como offers high-end Kenyan fusion cuisine in a stylish outdoor setting. Created by Swiss-trained chef Stephanie Kiragu, it is ideal for special lunches, refined dinners, and culinary-focused visitors.
Nifty Café & Wine Bar blends great coffee, comfort food, and live music. Known for its wings, pizzas, and weekend performances, it’s a favorite for relaxed social dining with scenic views.
Camellia Gardens is both a restaurant and an event venue, set within tea plantations. It’s popular for group outings, celebrations, and slow afternoons surrounded by nature.
Muna Tree Café, located at Brackenhurst, focuses on wholesome meals made with locally sourced produce. Its monthly farmers’ market adds to its community-centered appeal.
Brown’s Cheese Factory & Farm offers one of the most unique food experiences in Tigoni, combining cheese-making tours, tastings, and farm-to-table lunches.
Wellness & Spas
For visitors seeking rest and restoration, Tigoni offers a slower, more intentional approach to wellness.

Serenity Spa Tigoni is tucked away in the highlands, offering massages, spa treatments, and wellness experiences surrounded by nature. It is an ideal way to unwind after hiking, cycling, or a full day of exploration.
Dog-Friendly Activities in Tigoni
Tigoni is one of the most dog-friendly countryside destinations near Nairobi.
Ravenswood Walks are perfect for dogs, offering open trails, fresh air, and plenty of space to explore responsibly.
The Fig & Olive Restaurant warmly welcomes dogs, making it easy for pet owners to enjoy a full day out without leaving their companions behind.
Family-Friendly Activities in Tigoni
Families will find Tigoni refreshingly accessible and engaging for all ages.

Tea farm walks provide safe, open environments for children to explore nature while learning about farming and history. Cafés like The Fig & Olive and Muna Tree Café cater to families with outdoor space and relaxed settings.
Nature trails such as Brackenhurst are suitable for family walks, while farms, gardens, and open countryside allow children to experience nature away from screens and city congestion.
Why Visit Tigoni?

Tigoni is not a town in the traditional sense—it is a region, shaped by tea, history, landscape, and community. Its cool climate, rolling hills, and understated luxury make it one of Kenya’s most rewarding destinations for slow travel, nature, food, and wellness.

Tourism in Tigoni is emerging thoughtfully, and platforms like Experience Tigoni play a key role in curating authentic experiences, supporting local businesses, and helping visitors discover the region beyond surface-level attractions.
Whether you’re planning a day trip, a romantic escape, a family outing, or a curated multi-activity itinerary, Tigoni offers depth, beauty, and calm—just a short drive from Nairobi.

content.subheading: Ravenswood Waterfall
content.intro: Beyond its rolling tea plantations and cool highland air, Tigoni is home to a series of quiet, lesser-known waterfalls tucked within forests, tea estates, and countryside trails. These waterfalls are not large or commercialized, but that is precisely their charm. They are peaceful, scenic, and deeply connected to the landscape—best experienced slowly, on foot, and with local knowledge.
paragraphs: Beyond its rolling tea plantations and cool highland air, Tigoni is home to a series of quiet, lesser-known waterfalls tucked within forests, tea estates, and countryside trails. These waterfalls are not large or commercialized, but that is precisely their charm. They are peaceful, scenic, and deeply connected to the landscape—best experienced slowly, on foot, and with local knowledge.
closing: Here are the most notable waterfalls to explore in Tigoni.
Ravenswood Waterfall

The Ravenswood Waterfall is one of Tigoni’s most serene natural features, hidden within the expansive Ravenswood tea estate. Reached via gentle walking trails that pass through tea fields and forest edges, this waterfall is ideal for visitors seeking calm and privacy.

The surrounding area is lush and shaded, making it a popular stop during nature walks and guided hikes. The sound of flowing water, combined with the open tea landscape, creates a peaceful atmosphere perfect for photography, quiet reflection, or a short rest during a longer walk. It is also part of a dog-friendly trail, making it suitable for visitors exploring with pets.
Kawamwaki (Kwamwaki) Waterfall

The Kawamwaki Waterfall, also commonly referred to as Kwamwaki Waterfall, is one of Tigoni’s more adventurous waterfall spots. Reaching it involves walking through rural trails and sections of forest, giving visitors a more immersive countryside experience.

This waterfall feels raw and natural, surrounded by indigenous vegetation and open farmland. It is best explored with a local guide, especially after rainy seasons when paths can be slippery. For hikers and nature lovers looking for something off the beaten path, Kawamwaki offers a rewarding and authentic Tigoni experience.
Tigoni Tea Estate Waterfalls

Scattered within the Tigoni Tea Estate are small, seasonal waterfalls formed by natural streams cutting through the tea fields. These waterfalls are most active during the rainy months, when water flows steadily through the estate.

Visiting these waterfalls is often part of a tea estate walk, where guests experience both the agricultural landscape and its hidden natural features. The contrast between manicured tea rows and flowing water creates a striking visual experience unique to Tigoni.
Little Falls Tea Garden Waterfall

The Little Falls Tea Farm Waterfall lives up to its name—a small, charming waterfall set within a peaceful tea garden environment. It is easy to access and well suited for relaxed walks, families, and visitors looking for a gentle nature experience rather than a strenuous hike.

Surrounded by tea bushes and greenery, the waterfall adds a calming focal point to the farm walk and is especially picturesque after rainfall. It’s an ideal stop for casual explorers, photographers, and anyone seeking a quiet moment in nature.
Tips for Exploring Tigoni Waterfalls

Wear sturdy walking shoes, especially during rainy seasons. Consider guided walks for waterfalls like Kawamwaki, as trails can be less defined. Respect private tea estates by visiting only with permission or through organized tours, and always carry out what you bring in to preserve Tigoni’s natural beauty.

content.subheading: Picnic Sites in Tigoni: The Perfect Countryside Escape Near Nairobi
paragraphs: Tigoni is one of the most charming countryside destinations near Nairobi, known for its rolling tea plantations, cool highland climate, and peaceful landscapes. Sitting at approximately 2,100 metres above sea level, Tigoni offers a refreshing break from the city and has quietly become a favourite for relaxed outdoor experiences. Among its growing attractions, picnic sites stand out as one of the best ways to enjoy the area’s natural beauty.

Whether you are planning a family outing, a romantic date, or a slow weekend escape with friends, Tigoni offers a variety of picnic spots set within lush greenery, riverbanks, waterfalls, and manicured gardens. Below are some of the best picnic sites in Tigoni, each offering a distinct experience while staying true to the area’s serene countryside charm.
closing: Twin Rivers Resort – Nature, Adventure & Family Fun

Twin Rivers Resort is one of the newest and most popular picnic and activity destinations in Tigoni. Set within expansive green grounds near flowing rivers, the resort has quickly won the hearts of locals and visitors alike.

It is particularly well-suited for families and groups, offering a mix of relaxation and adventure. Visitors can enjoy shaded picnic areas surrounded by nature while children and thrill-seekers take part in activities such as zip-lining and other outdoor games. The wide open spaces make it ideal for day trips, birthdays, and casual group outings.
Twin Rivers Resort combines the calm of Tigoni’s natural environment with structured activities, making it one of the most versatile picnic destinations in the area.
Best for: Families, groups, active day trips
Experience: Picnic grounds, outdoor activities, riverside setting
Green’s Green Retreat – Exclusive Picnics with a Touch of Luxury

Green’s Green Retreat is known for its exclusivity and carefully curated experiences. Tucked away in Tigoni’s green countryside, this retreat offers a more private and refined picnic setting compared to busier public spaces.

One of its standout features is the water slide, which adds a playful element to the serene environment. The grounds are well maintained, spacious, and ideal for visitors looking for a quiet, controlled atmosphere where comfort and privacy are prioritised.
Green’s Green Retreat is particularly popular for couples, small private groups, and those seeking a more premium picnic experience without crowds.
Best for: Couples, private groups, relaxed luxury
Experience: Exclusive picnic spaces, water features, quiet countryside
Waterfalls Inn – Picnicking by the Water

Waterfalls Inn offers one of the most naturally scenic picnic experiences in Tigoni. As the name suggests, the property is known for its waterfall, which creates a calm and refreshing backdrop for a day outdoors.

The sound of flowing water, combined with lush vegetation and serene grounds, makes this an ideal location for slow picnics, quiet conversations, and moments of reflection. It is especially appealing to visitors who appreciate natural landscapes and prefer minimal distractions.
Waterfalls Inn is well suited for relaxed picnics, photography, and nature lovers seeking a peaceful setting within Tigoni.
Best for: Nature lovers, quiet outings, photography
Experience: Waterfall views, tranquil grounds, natural atmosphere
Naiposha Gardens – Aesthetic & Budget-Friendly Picnic Experiences

Naiposha Gardens has become increasingly popular for its visually appealing picnic setups and affordability. Designed with aesthetics in mind, the gardens offer neatly arranged picnic spaces that are perfect for casual outings, small celebrations, and social gatherings.

Visitors can enjoy well-maintained lawns and thoughtfully designed picnic areas without the high costs often associated with curated outdoor experiences. This makes Naiposha Gardens a great option for students, young couples, and anyone seeking a simple yet beautiful picnic in Tigoni.
Its flexibility and budget-friendly options have made it a favourite for relaxed weekend plans and spontaneous day trips from Nairobi.
Best for: Casual picnics, aesthetic setups, budget-conscious visitors
Experience: Manicured gardens, styled picnic spaces, affordability
Why Tigoni Is Ideal for Picnics

Tigoni’s appeal as a picnic destination goes beyond individual venues. The region’s cool climate, fresh air, and expansive tea plantations create a naturally calming environment that enhances any outdoor experience. Unlike crowded urban parks, Tigoni offers space, quiet, and a slower pace that allows visitors to truly unwind.

Its close proximity to Nairobi makes it ideal for half-day or full-day trips, while the variety of picnic venues ensures there is something for every type of visitor — from adventure seekers to those simply looking to lay out a blanket and enjoy the view.
Planning Your Picnic in Tigoni
When planning a picnic in Tigoni, it is advisable to:

• Confirm booking requirements in advance, especially for weekends
  • Arrive early to fully enjoy the day and avoid peak-hour traffic
  • Carry warm clothing, as temperatures can drop in the afternoon
  • Respect the natural environment and follow venue guidelines
Experience Tigoni the Local Way

Picnicking in Tigoni is more than just an outdoor meal — it is a chance to experience the landscape, lifestyle, and calm that define this highland region. From riverside adventures and waterfall views to exclusive retreats and garden picnics, Tigoni continues to emerge as one of the most rewarding countryside escapes near Nairobi.
For more curated experiences, local insights, and trusted recommendations, Tigoni Guide helps you discover Tigoni beyond the obvious.

content.subheading: Redhill Go-Karting – High-Speed Fun in Tigoni’s Tea Highlands
paragraphs: If you’re looking to add adrenaline to your Tigoni adventure, Redhill Go-Karting delivers speed, scenery, and excitement in equal measure. Located in the scenic Redhill area off Limuru Road, this outdoor karting track offers a rare combination of thrill racing and panoramic views of tea plantations, making it one of the most exciting family-friendly activities near Tigoni.
Whether you’re planning a weekend outing, a birthday celebration, or a team-building experience, Redhill Go-Karting is designed for both first-time drivers and seasoned speed lovers.
closing: 📍 Location & Accessibility

• Location: Redhill area, off Limuru Road
  • Nearby Landmark: Next to Bustani Gardens, Kianjogu
  • Distance from Nairobi: About 40 minutes’ drive
The track is easily accessible by private car and tour vans, with ample parking on site.
⏰ Operating Hours

• Open Days: Tuesday – Sunday
  • Karting Hours: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
  • Closed: Mondays
  • On-Site Restaurant: Open until 9:00 PM
This makes it easy to combine racing with lunch, sunset views, or dinner.
💰 Go-Karting Prices (2025)
Rates per person (effective August 12, 2025):

• 10 Minutes: Ksh 3,000
  • 20 Minutes: Ksh 5,000
  • 30 Minutes: Ksh 7,000
🌅 Early Bird Special
• 10% discount for guests arriving between 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM
Ideal for families, school holidays, and group bookings looking for better value.
🏎️ What to Expect on the Track
🛡️ Safety & Professional Setup
Redhill Go-Karting prioritizes safety and professionalism:

• Helmets and racing suits provided
  • Pre-race safety briefing for all drivers
  • Well-maintained outdoor track
Even beginners can race confidently with staff guidance.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family-Friendly Experience
Redhill Go-Karting is known as a great venue for kids and families, with:

• Controlled racing environment
  • Birthday party hosting
  • Group and corporate team-building sessions
Children, teens, and adults can all participate, making it a rare activity everyone enjoys together.
♿ Accessibility
The facility is wheelchair accessible, including:

• Entrance
  • Parking
  • Restrooms
This makes it one of the more inclusive adventure attractions near Tigoni.
🍽️ On-Site Restaurant & Views
After racing, guests can relax at the on-site restaurant, which stays open until 9:00 PM. It’s a perfect spot to:

• Unwind after the track
  • Enjoy food with countryside views
  • Watch the sunset over tea plantations
🎯 Best For

• Families with kids & teens
  • Birthday parties & celebrations
  • Corporate team-building
  • Adrenaline seekers
  • Weekend getaways near Nairobi
💡 Visitor Tips

• ✔ Arrive early for the Early Bird discount
  • ✔ Wear comfortable clothes & closed shoes
  • ✔ Combine karting with nearby attractions in Tigoni
  • ✔ Book ahead for groups or birthdays
  • ✔ Stay for sunset and dinner at the restaurant
❓ Frequently Asked Questions (SEO)
Where is Redhill Go-Karting located?
Redhill Go-Karting is located in the Redhill area off Limuru Road, next to Bustani Gardens in Kianjogu, near Tigoni.
How much does go-karting cost at Redhill?
Prices range from Ksh 3,000 to Ksh 7,000 per person, depending on session length.
Is Redhill Go-Karting suitable for kids?
Yes. It is a family-friendly venue, popular for kids’ birthday parties and group activities.
Are safety gear and instructions provided?
Yes. Helmets, racing suits, and a full safety briefing are provided before racing.
Is Redhill Go-Karting wheelchair accessible?
Yes. The facility has accessible entrances, parking, and restrooms.

content.subheading: Brackenhurst Conference and Retreat Centre – A Peaceful Sanctuary in Tigoni
paragraphs: Set amid rolling tea fields and restored indigenous forest, Brackenhurst Conference and Retreat Centre is one of Tigoni’s most respected retreat destinations. Located about 20 km northwest of Nairobi, this Christian-based centre offers a rare blend of serenity, sustainability, and professional hospitality, making it ideal for conferences, retreats, team building, and quiet personal getaways.
With its cool highland climate, bird-filled forest trails, and thoughtfully designed facilities, Brackenhurst feels worlds away from the city—yet remains easily accessible.
closing: 📍 Location & Contact Information

• Address: Limuru–Banana Road, Tigoni, Limuru – Kiambu County, Kenya
  • Phone: +254 724 256 721 / +254 748 199 552
  • Email: bookings@brackenhurst.com
  • Setting: Tea plantations and indigenous forest, Tigoni
A private vehicle or taxi is recommended, especially if combining your visit with other Tigoni attractions.
🏨 Facilities & Amenities
🧠 Conference & Meeting Spaces
Brackenhurst is widely known for hosting corporate, church, NGO, and leadership retreats, offering:

• Multiple meeting halls and breakout rooms
  • Capacity for small groups to 200+ delegates
  • High-speed Wi-Fi
  • Audio-visual equipment, sound, and lighting systems
The calm environment enhances focus, reflection, and collaboration.
🛏️ Accommodation
Guests can choose from a variety of well-maintained lodging options:

• Single, double, triple & quad rooms
  • Family suites and private cottages
  • En-suite bathrooms
  • Solar-heated water
The accommodation is simple, comfortable, and designed for rest rather than luxury.
🍽️ Dining & Café

• Main Restaurant: Buffet-style local and international meals
  • Muna Tree Café: Light meals, snacks, and coffee
  • Alcohol-free property, aligning with its Christian ethos
Many ingredients are sourced directly from the centre’s own gardens.
🌿 Activities & Experiences
Brackenhurst offers more than just meetings—it’s a nature-based retreat:

• Guided forest walks & ecotours
  • Bird watching (rich biodiversity in restored forest)
  • Hiking trails through tea fields
  • Mountain biking
  • Children’s playground
  • Tennis & basketball courts
It’s especially popular with families, retreat groups, and wellness-focused travelers.
🌱 Sustainability & Conservation
One of Brackenhurst’s defining strengths is its deep commitment to environmental stewardship:

• Restoration of a large indigenous forest reserve
  • Use of solar energy systems
  • On-site farming to supply fresh produce
  • Eco-conscious building and land use practices
This makes it a model destination for eco-retreats and responsible travel in Kenya.
⭐ Why Visitors Love Brackenhurst
Guests consistently highlight:

• The quiet, prayerful atmosphere
  • Beautifully maintained grounds
  • Cool, refreshing Tigoni climate
  • Friendly, attentive staff
  • Ideal balance between isolation and accessibility
It’s often described as a place to slow down, reflect, and reset.
🎯 Best For

• Conferences & workshops
  • Church retreats & spiritual getaways
  • Corporate team building
  • Family retreats
  • Nature lovers & bird watchers
  • Quiet weekend escapes from Nairobi
💡 Visitor Tips

• ✔ Book accommodation and conference spaces in advance
  • ✔ Bring walking shoes for forest trails
  • ✔ Dress warmly—Tigoni evenings can be cool
  • ✔ Combine your visit with nearby tea farms or hikes
  • ✔ Ideal for digital detox and reflection
❓ FAQs (SEO Friendly)
Where is Brackenhurst Conference and Retreat Centre located?
It is located in Tigoni, Limuru, along Limuru–Banana Road in Kiambu County, about 30 minutes from Nairobi.
Is Brackenhurst suitable for conferences and retreats?
Yes, it is one of the leading retreat and conference centres near Nairobi, hosting groups of up to 200 delegates.
Does Brackenhurst offer accommodation?
Yes, the centre provides multiple room options including family suites and cottages.
Is Brackenhurst eco-friendly?
Yes. The centre emphasizes sustainability through forest restoration, solar energy, and organic farming.
Is alcohol allowed at Brackenhurst?
No. The property is alcohol-free.

content.subheading: Project Pamoja – A Community Learning & Play Hub in Tigoni
paragraphs: Project Pamoja is a community-centered hub located at Naishola Gardens in Tigoni, Limuru, created as a safe, inspiring space for children and families. Designed around the principles of sportsmanship, emotional enrichment, and social-emotional learning, Project Pamoja blends play, education, and community values in a calm, nature-rich environment.
closing: 🌱 What Project Pamoja Offers
⚽ Sports & Physical Development

Project Pamoja runs a Sports Academy that hosts structured athletic activities and community tournaments, including football events such as the Fall Classic Football Tournament, bringing together local schools like Limuru International School and SABIS. These programs emphasize teamwork, discipline, and fair play rather than competition alone.
📚 Safe Learning & Creative Space

At the heart of the center is the “backYARD” learning space—an after-school safe haven where children engage in child-led activities, creative exploration, and hands-on learning. The environment encourages curiosity, decision-making, and intellectual confidence.
🌞 Holiday Camps & Experiential Learning
During school holidays, Project Pamoja hosts premium programs such as Camp Rafiki, an experiential camp focused on:

• Independence and leadership
  • Personal reflection and confidence-building
  • Social skills and collaboration
These camps are designed to nurture the whole child beyond traditional classroom learning.
🤝 Community Values & Life Skills
Project Pamoja places strong emphasis on:

• Social justice and empathy
  • Conflict resolution
  • Shared responsibility
Children participate in daily work jobs, teaching accountability, cooperation, and respect for community spaces.
📍 Location

• Where: Naishola Gardens, Tigoni – Limuru, Kenya
  • Setting: Lush gardens within Tigoni’s tea-growing highlands
⭐ Why Project Pamoja Matters

Project Pamoja stands out as more than just a children’s center—it is a values-driven community space where learning, play, and emotional growth are intentionally woven together. It’s especially ideal for families in Tigoni and Limuru seeking safe after-school programs, meaningful holiday camps, and holistic child development.

gridPosts.seoTitle: Play Padel in Tigoni – Destination Padel at The Fig & Olive

content.subheading: Play Padel in Tigoni – Destination Padel at The Fig & Olive
paragraphs: Padel is quickly becoming one of Kenya’s favorite social sports, and Tigoni is now home to one of the most scenic places to play it. Destination Padel, located at The Fig & Olive along Raini Goldhandas Road, offers modern, all-weather padel courts set against Tigoni’s cool highland air and tea-growing countryside. Whether you’re a beginner trying padel for the first time or a regular player looking for competitive games, this spot blends sport, community, and lifestyle beautifully.
closing: 📍 Location & Booking

Destination Padel is designed for year-round play and comfort:

⭐ Why Play Padel in Tigoni?

gridPosts.title: Sensory Garden Tigoni – A Unique Farm & Tea Experience for All Five Senses
gridPosts.seoTitle: Sensory Garden Tigoni – A Unique Farm & Tea Experience for All Five Senses
gridPosts.excerpt: Unleash your senses at the Sensory Garden Tigoni, an immersive farm and tea experience just 30 minutes from Nairobi.
gridPosts.author: Tigoni

paragraphs: Nestled within the lush tea landscapes of Tigoni, the Sensory Garden at Gathoni Farm offers one of the most immersive and family-friendly farm experiences near Nairobi. Just 30 minutes from the city, this tranquil destination blends tea farming, nature education, sensory exploration, and child-friendly activities, making it ideal for families, schools, couples, and nature lovers.
Unlike traditional farm tours, the Sensory Garden is intentionally designed to engage all five senses—sight, smell, touch, taste, and sound—creating a memorable and educational outdoor experience.
closing: 📍 Location & Accessibility

• Location: Gathoni Farm, along Limuru Road, Tigoni
  • Area Code: 00217, Ithanji, Kenya
  • Drive Time: Approximately 30–40 minutes from Nairobi CBD
  • Road Access: Fully accessible by private car and tour vans
The farm is set deep within tea plantations, offering cool weather, scenic views, and clean countryside air.
⏰ Opening Hours & Tour Times

• Farm Open: Daily, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
  • Adult Tours:
    • Wednesday to Saturday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM
  • Kids’ Sensory Tours:
    • Saturdays: 9:00 AM – 11:00 AM
⏳ Tours are guided and time-specific, so punctual arrival is recommended.
Advance booking is encouraged, especially for school groups, families, and weekend visits.
🌿 What to Expect: Experiences & Activities
🍃 Tea Farm Tour (Farm-to-Cup Experience)
Guests are guided through the tea fields to learn:

• How tea is grown and harvested
  • The difference between tea varieties
  • Hands-on tea picking
  • Processing basics and sustainability practices
This is both educational and interactive, suitable for adults and children alike.
🌸 Sensory Garden Walk
The heart of the experience is a guided walk through a carefully curated sensory garden filled with aromatic plants and herbs.
You’ll encounter:

• Lavender
  • Rosemary
  • Lemon verbena
  • Mint
  • Indigenous herbs
Guests are encouraged to touch, smell, observe, and taste, making the experience deeply engaging and calming.
☕ Herbal Tea Tasting
After the garden walk, visitors enjoy:

• Freshly brewed herbal teas from the garden
  • Homemade biscuits
  • A relaxed, communal tasting session
This moment often becomes a favorite for adults seeking calm and connection.
🐇 Animal Interaction (Perfect for Kids)
Children especially love the hands-on animal experiences:

• Petting rabbits
  • Feeding sheep, chickens, and ducks
These interactions foster curiosity, empathy, and joy in a safe environment.
🌾 Additional Activities Available
Depending on the day and group type, visitors may also enjoy:

• Donkey cart rides
  • Potting and planting activities
  • Composting and sustainability lessons
  • Fishing at Lake Tigoni (via Naiposha Gardens)
  • Picnicking on the farm grounds
🌳 Naishola & Naiposha Gardens
Gathoni Farm is home to two scenic garden spaces:

• Naishola Gardens: Open lawns, ideal for relaxation and picnics
  • Naiposha Gardens: Overlooks Lake Tigoni, perfect for photography, fishing, and aesthetic picnics
Visitors are welcome to carry their own picnic lunch, snacks, and drinks, making it a great half-day family outing.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Who Is the Sensory Garden Best For?

• Families with young children
  • School educational trips
  • Nature and wellness lovers
  • Couples seeking a calm outdoor date
  • Visitors looking for a unique experience near Nairobi
💡 Tips for Visitors

• ✔ Book in advance, especially on weekends
  • ✔ Dress warmly (Tigoni is cool)
  • ✔ Carry snacks or picnic food
  • ✔ Bring a camera for the tea fields & lake views
  • ✔ Arrive early for guided tours

gridPosts.title: Tigoni Equestrian Centre – Horse Riding in the Heart of Tigoni
gridPosts.seoTitle: Tigoni Equestrian Centre – Horse Riding in the Heart of Tigoni
gridPosts.excerpt: Discover Tigoni Equestrian Centre, a serene horse-riding haven nestled in Tigoni's green hills, offering scenic experiences near Nairobi.

paragraphs: Tigoni Equestrian Centre is a peaceful horse-riding destination tucked into the rolling green hills of Tigoni, Limuru, offering one of the most scenic equestrian experiences near Nairobi. Surrounded by tea plantations, forests, and open countryside, the centre is ideal for families, beginners, and experienced riders looking to connect with nature on horseback.
Horse Riding Experiences

The centre offers guided trail rides that take riders through quiet farm paths, tea estates, and gentle hills, allowing you to enjoy Tigoni’s cool climate and stunning landscapes at a relaxed pace. Trails are suitable for all skill levels, making it a great introduction to horse riding for first-timers.
Riding Lessons

For those looking to learn or improve their skills, Tigoni Equestrian Centre provides professional riding lessons focused on balance, control, and confidence. Lessons are available for both children and adults, using well-trained, calm horses that are ideal for learning.
Family-Friendly Atmosphere

Known for its warm and welcoming environment, the centre is popular with families and kids. Safety is a priority, and instructors ensure riders are comfortable before heading out on the trails. It’s a perfect weekend activity for parents looking to introduce children to outdoor adventures.
closing: Why Visit Tigoni Equestrian Centre

• Scenic horse riding through tea plantations
  • Suitable for beginners and experienced riders
  • Professional lessons for kids and adults
  • Calm, well-trained horses
  • A relaxing outdoor experience close to Nairobi
Perfect Day Trip from Nairobi
Located less than an hour from Nairobi, Tigoni Equestrian Centre makes for an easy day trip or weekend escape, combining adventure, relaxation, and fresh highland air.

Whether you’re seeking a peaceful ride through nature, a fun family activity, or structured riding lessons, Tigoni Equestrian Centre offers a memorable and authentic equestrian experience in one of Kenya’s most beautiful countryside settings. 🐎🌿

gridPosts.title: Tigoni Farmers Market (Organic Farmers Market)
gridPosts.seoTitle: Tigoni Farmers Market (Organic Farmers Market)
gridPosts.excerpt: Experience the vibrant Tigoni Organic Farmers Market, held every Saturday from 10 AM to 5 PM at Little Falls Tea Garden.

content.subheading: Tigoni Farmers Market (Organic Farmers Market)
paragraphs: The Organic Farmers Market, Tigoni is a vibrant weekly market held every Saturday from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM at Little Falls Tea Garden along Riara Ridge, Limuru. Set within rolling tea plantations and near a scenic waterfall, it’s one of the most relaxed and authentic ways to experience Tigoni’s local food culture.
closing: 📍 Location & Hours

• Location: Little Falls Tea Garden, Riara Ridge, Limuru, Tigoni
  • Day: Every Saturday
  • Time: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
🛍️ What You’ll Find
The market brings together farmers, makers, and artisans from Tigoni and surrounding areas:

• Fresh Organic Produce: Seasonal vegetables, fruits, herbs, and farm-fresh eggs—often harvested the same morning.
  • Artisan Foods: Homemade bread and pastries, organic honey, spices, fermented foods, and natural beverages.
  • Wellness & Lifestyle Products: Natural skincare, plant seedlings, eco-friendly cleaning products, organic flours, and superfoods like seamoss gel.
  • Crafts & Handmade Goods: Locally made items that reflect Tigoni’s creative community.
👨‍👩‍👧 Family-Friendly Experience
The market is ideal for families, offering open green spaces for kids, light music, short nature walks, and views of the nearby waterfall—making it more than just a shopping stop.
🌿 Why Visit
The Tigoni Farmers Market is perfect for slow weekends, supporting local farmers, enjoying clean food, and soaking in the calm highland atmosphere—just a short drive from Nairobi.

gridPosts.title: Liquor Library Tigoni – Premium Drinks & Scenic Hangouts in the Highlands

content.subheading: Liquor Library Tigoni – Premium Drinks & Scenic Hangouts in the Highlands
paragraphs: Liquor Library Tigoni is a premium liquor retail outlet and outdoor social destination set in the lush tea-growing highlands of Limuru, just 30 km from Nairobi. Known for its rustic countryside charm, it’s a favorite spot for weekend getaways, social gatherings, and unique events in a relaxed, scenic environment.
closing: 📍 Location & Accessibility

• Location: Limuru Highlands, Tigoni, Kenya
  • Distance: ~30 km from Nairobi (~45 minutes by car)
  • Operating Days: Typically Thursday–Sunday, with special events throughout the year
  • Private Bookings: Available for birthdays, corporate events, and large parties (up to 1,000 guests)
🍷 Key Experiences & Events

• Open Wine Sunsets: Seasonal sunset events featuring wine tastings, smooth music, and stunning views. Popular editions occur in May, August, and November.
  • The Meat Up Fest: A BBQ festival with unlimited grilled meats (beef, lamb, pork, chicken), a local marketplace, and live music. Next edition scheduled for March 1, 2025.
  • Casual Picnics & Hangouts: Visitors can enjoy drinks and small meals on picnic blankets or camping chairs, surrounded by lawns, streams, and a playground for children.
  • Private Events: Ideal for large celebrations or corporate team-building events, with catering options available.
🍽️ Amenities & Policies

• Food & Drink: On-site meals include grilled chicken, pork, and traditional Kenyan dishes like ugali and mukimo.
  • Redeemable Tickets: Many events use tickets where a portion of the entry fee (e.g., KES 1,200 of KES 1,500) can be redeemed for drinks.
  • Pet-Friendly: Dogs are welcome on the property.
  • No BYOB: All alcoholic drinks must be purchased on-site; outside beverages are not permitted.
🌄 Why Visit

Liquor Library Tigoni is more than a liquor store—it’s a destination to relax, socialize, and enjoy the scenic highlands. Whether attending a festival, enjoying a wine sunset, or planning a private event, visitors are treated to a blend of countryside charm, curated drinks, and memorable experiences.

gridPosts.title: Teagoni Tea Farm – A Hands-On Family-Owned Tea Experience in Tigoni
gridPosts.seoTitle: Teagoni Tea Farm – A Hands-On Family-Owned Tea Experience in Tigoni
gridPosts.excerpt: Discover Teagoni Tea Farm, a highly-rated, family-owned estate in Tigoni, Kenya, offering a unique hands-on tea experience.

content.subheading: Teagoni Tea Farm – A Hands-On Family-Owned Tea Experience in Tigoni
paragraphs: Teagoni Tea Farm is a highly-rated, family-owned tea estate located in Tigoni, Kenya. Known for its immersive tours and homemade meals, it offers visitors a chance to experience the entire journey of tea, from the fields to your cup, all within a scenic, highland setting.
closing: 🍃 Experiences & Activities
Teagoni Tea Farm provides an educational and hands-on experience, perfect for tea lovers, families, and anyone curious about Kenyan tea culture:

• Guided Tea Tours: Stroll through lush tea plantations while learning about cultivation, harvesting, and traditional plucking methods.
  • Leaf-to-Cup Tea Making Class: Participate in the entire process of tea production—from picking leaves to brewing fresh tea.
  • Tea Tastings & Homemade Meals: Enjoy guided tastings of different teas and a farm-fresh homemade lunch using local produce. Vegan options are available.
  • Scenic Gardens: Dine or relax in the farm’s picturesque gardens, surrounded by rolling tea fields.
  • Family-Owned Authenticity: Guests often highlight the warm hospitality and intimate atmosphere, making it feel like a homegrown Kenyan experience.
🌟 Why Visit

Teagoni Tea Farm is ideal for travelers seeking authentic, immersive, and educational experiences in the Kenyan highlands. It combines hands-on learning, scenic landscapes, and delicious homemade meals for a memorable day trip just under an hour from Nairobi.

gridPosts.title: Kawamwaki Farm – Eco-Retreat, Horse Riding & Nature Tours in Tigoni
gridPosts.seoTitle: Kawamwaki Farm – Eco-Retreat, Horse Riding & Nature Tours in Tigoni
gridPosts.excerpt: Discover Kawamwaki Farm, Tigoni's premier eco-retreat and working estate, where lush tea plantations meet thrilling horse riding and immersive nature tours.

paragraphs: Kawamwaki Farm is a multi-functional working estate and eco-retreat located in Riara Ridge, Tigoni, Limuru, Kenya. Set amid lush tea plantations, indigenous forests, waterfalls, and a dam, the farm blends sustainable agriculture, outdoor adventures, and educational experiences, making it ideal for families, nature lovers, and adventure seekers.
closing: 🍃 Experiences & Activities
1. Horse Riding & Equestrian Trails

• Guided horse rides take visitors through tea plantations, forests, and waterfalls, suitable for beginners and experienced riders.
  • Pony rides are available for children.
  • All riders are provided with safety gear, including helmets, and guided by trained instructors.
  • Typical price: ~KES 2,000 per hour per person (prices may vary).
2. Nature & Outdoor Adventures

• Hiking & Walking: Explore trails like the “Kawamwaki Marvel,” forest paths, and plantation ridges.
  • Picnics: Scenic areas, especially near the A-frame studio grounds, perfect for family outings.
  • Bird Watching: The diverse environment attracts multiple bird species.
  • Waterfalls & Dam: Visit natural waterfalls and the farm’s dam for photography and relaxation.
3. Organic Farming & Sustainable Agriculture

• The farm produces organic vegetables, herbs, fruits, and free-range eggs, available for purchase or local delivery.
  • A small dairy produces fresh whole milk.
  • Visitors can learn about tree planting, composting, and eco-conscious farming practices.
4. Accommodation & Retreats

• Self-catering lodging in a 1920s farmhouse and studio cottages is available for overnight stays.
  • The farm hosts yoga retreats, workshops, and artist residencies, providing a serene environment for creativity and relaxation.
  • Venue hire is possible for weddings, family events, and corporate retreats.
🌟 Why Visit Kawamwaki Farm

Kawamwaki Farm is perfect for those seeking an eco-friendly, immersive countryside experience. It combines adventure, wellness, and learning, offering horseback riding, farm-to-table experiences, and nature exploration all within a tranquil highland setting just outside Nairobi.

gridPosts.seoTitle: Coomete Farm – Tea Plantation, Eco-Tourism & Retreats in Tigoni

content.subheading: Coomete Farm – Tea Plantation, Eco-Tourism & Retreats in Tigoni
paragraphs: Coomete Farm is a 100-acre, family-owned agricultural estate located in Tigoni, Limuru, Kenya, with a legacy spanning over 76 years. Renowned for its high-quality tea and specialty produce, the farm also doubles as an eco-tourism destination, offering immersive countryside experiences for visitors seeking nature, relaxation, and hands-on farm activities.
closing: 📍 Location & Contact

• Location: Tigoni, Limuru, Kiambu County, Kenya
  • Distance from Nairobi: Approximately 40–60 minutes by car.
  • Booking & Contact: Direct inquiries for tours or accommodation can be made via their official channels (phone/email via website).
🍃 Experiences & Activities
1. Tea Plantation Tours

• Visitors can take guided tea farm walks, learning about tea cultivation, harvesting, and local processing.
  • Engage in hands-on tea picking, ideal for families, students, or agro-tourism enthusiasts.
2. Green’s Green Retreat – Countryside Accommodations
Coomete Farm hosts Green’s Green Retreat, a serene countryside getaway:

• Cabin Alpha: An A-frame cabin retreat for 6–8 guests.
  • Villa Africana & Villa Toboga: Spacious villas with modern amenities for small groups or families.

• E-Bike Tours: Explore the farm and surrounding landscapes on guided electric bike rides.

  • Water slide: A free waterslide if you have paid your picnic fees
4. Specialty Agriculture & Produce

• Core Crop: Tea, harvested daily and processed locally in Tigoni.
  • Specialty Vegetables: Brussels sprouts, parsnips, white kohlrabi, frozen strawberries, and kienyeji eggs.
  • Suppliers to Premium Markets: Products are supplied to high-end retailers like Greenspoon and Zucchini Food Market.
